purpose: Design and implement an Ananta-native optimized coding and shell worker that combines the best ideas from Aider, OpenCode, ShellGPT, GitHub Copilot CLI and similar tools while remaining policy-aware, RAG-aware, artifact-first, approval-gated and Hub-controlled. External tools may be used as optional adapters or inspiration, but the worker core must be owned by Ananta.
goal: Create a native Ananta worker that can plan code changes, propose patches, run bounded tests, generate shell command plans, create artifacts, verify results and optionally invoke external coding tools through controlled adapters without bypassing Hub policy, approval or audit.
non_goals: Do not fork ShellGPT, Aider, OpenCode or Copilot CLI as the core Ananta worker.
Do not make any external coding CLI a required dependency for the OSS golden path.
Do not let the worker create its own orchestration, approval or policy decisions.
Do not execute arbitrary shell commands without policy evaluation and approval where required.
Do not apply patches directly to the main working tree without explicit approval and trace metadata.
Do not copy incompatible licensed code from other projects without review.
Do not turn this worker into a separate autonomous product disconnected from Ananta contracts.
success_criteria: Worker accepts Task, ContextEnvelope, capability, policy decision and execution constraints from the Hub.
Worker can produce PlanArtifact, PatchArtifact, CommandPlanArtifact, TestResultArtifact and VerificationArtifact.
Worker can propose code patches using bounded context and codecompass/rag_helper retrieval references.
Worker can apply patches only in an isolated worktree/sandbox and only after approval when policy requires it.
Worker can run allow-listed tests/commands and capture stdout/stderr/exit codes as artifacts.
Worker can generate ShellGPT-style command explanations/plans without executing by default.
Worker supports optional adapters for Aider, OpenCode, ShellGPT-like command helpers and GitHub Copilot CLI without making them core dependencies.
All worker results include provenance, hashes, trace IDs, model/provider metadata where applicable and reproducibility hints.
E2E dogfood tests prove a tiny repo patch flow, failing-test repair flow, denied-command flow and approval-gated apply flow.
architecture_guardrails: Hub owns Goal -> Plan -> Task -> Execution -> Verification -> Artifact orchestration.
Worker executes only tasks delegated by the Hub and must return artifacts, not hidden side effects.
Worker never evaluates final policy itself; it consumes policy decisions and enforces constraints passed by Hub.
Worker may perform local safety checks, but local checks cannot loosen Hub policy.
All file writes happen in an explicit workspace/worktree and are represented as patch artifacts before final apply.
Shell command execution is default-deny unless allow-listed or explicitly approved through Hub policy.
RAG/code context must arrive through ContextEnvelope or approved retrieval services with provenance.
External adapters are optional, isolated and capability-declared.
External tool output is untrusted and must be parsed, validated and converted into Ananta artifacts.
The worker must degrade cleanly when no LLM or external coding tool is configured.

milestones.title: Shell command planning and execution controls
Model provider and external tool adapters

tasks.title: Ananta native worker architecture define
acceptance_criteria: Document defines worker as Ananta-native, Hub-controlled and artifact-first.
Document distinguishes native worker core, shell mode, coding mode and optional external adapters.
Document explains why Aider/OpenCode/ShellGPT/Copilot CLI are inspiration or optional adapters, not the worker core.
Document maps worker operations to Policy, Approval, Audit, ContextEnvelope and TraceBundle.

tasks.title: Worker execution request schema define
acceptance_criteria: Schema includes task_id, goal_id, trace_id, capability_id, mode, ContextEnvelope reference, policy decision reference, approval reference, workspace constraints and requested outputs.
Schema distinguishes plan_only, patch_propose, patch_apply, command_plan, command_execute, test_run and verify modes.
Schema supports required approvals and denied/degraded states.
Schema does not allow unbounded raw repo access or unlabelled context blobs.

tasks.title: Worker result and artifact schemas define
acceptance_criteria: Result schema captures status, produced artifacts, stdout/stderr refs, exit code, error class, trace IDs and reproducibility metadata.
Patch artifact includes target files, diff, patch hash, base commit/ref, risk classification and expected effects.
Command plan artifact includes command, explanation, risk, required approval, working directory and expected effects.
Test/verification artifacts capture command, exit code, output summary, failure hints and links to logs.

tasks.title: Worker capability pack define
acceptance_criteria: Capabilities include worker.code.plan, worker.patch.propose, worker.patch.apply, worker.test.run, worker.command.plan, worker.command.execute and worker.verify.result.
Patch apply and command execute are approval-required or default-deny by default.
Capabilities align with todo.security.json and todo.more-generic.json conventions.
Policy pack denies unknown or unclassified operations.

tasks.title: Worker workspace model define
acceptance_criteria: Workspace model defines allowed roots, temporary worktrees, read-only inputs, writable outputs and cleanup behavior.
Model forbids direct writes to main working tree unless explicitly approved and routed through apply logic.
Workspace constraints include max files, max bytes, allowed commands and allowed output paths.
Document explains local developer usage versus CI/test usage.

tasks.title: Git worktree sandbox implement
acceptance_criteria: Worker can create isolated temp worktree for patch proposal and test runs.
Worker records base ref/commit and changed files.
Worker cleanup is safe and testable.
Tests verify patch operations do not modify the original working tree unless apply is explicitly invoked.

tasks.title: Patch planner and diff builder implement
acceptance_criteria: Patch planner can create a plan artifact before any file write.
Diff builder produces unified diffs from sandbox changes.
Patch artifact includes patch hash, file list, expected effects and RAG/context refs.
Tests cover add, modify, delete, malformed patch and empty patch cases.

tasks.title: Approval-gated patch apply implement
acceptance_criteria: Patch apply requires matching approval reference when policy requires approval.
Approval binding includes patch hash, task id, capability id and context hash.
Stale, mismatched or missing approval prevents apply.
Apply result records changed files and artifact references.

tasks.title: Patch rollback and failure handling add
acceptance_criteria: Worker can report partial apply failures without hiding changed state.
Worker can provide rollback guidance or reset sandbox state where safe.
Failure artifacts include enough detail for repair planning.
Tests cover patch conflict and invalid target state.

tasks.title: RAG-aware file selection service implement
acceptance_criteria: Service selects candidate files/symbols from ContextEnvelope and codecompass/rag_helper refs.
Selection returns reasons and source provenance.
Selection respects max file/count/byte limits.
Selection can degrade to explicit user-provided files when RAG is unavailable.

tasks.title: Coding prompt assembly implement
acceptance_criteria: Prompt assembly includes task, constraints, selected files, relevant symbols, policies, expected output schema and forbidden actions.
Prompt assembly records prompt template version and context hash.
Prompt does not include unbounded full repo dumps.
Tests verify redaction and bounded context behavior.

tasks.title: Native model-backed patch proposal implement
acceptance_criteria: Native proposer can call configured model provider to produce structured patch proposal or edit plan.
LLM output is parsed, schema-validated and never blindly applied.
Invalid output produces degraded/repairable artifact rather than hidden failure.
Tests use deterministic mocked model output.

tasks.title: Deterministic no-LLM coding fallback add
acceptance_criteria: Worker can produce useful analysis/plan artifacts when no model is configured.
Fallback does not claim semantic patch generation when no model is used.
Result metadata records llm_used=false and fallback_reason.
Tests verify no-LLM mode remains schema-valid and honest.

tasks.title: Shell command plan mode implement
acceptance_criteria: Worker can generate ShellGPT-style command explanations and command plans as artifacts.
Command plans include explanation, risk, working directory, expected effects and required approval state.
Command planning does not execute commands by default.
Tests cover safe command, risky command and malformed request.

tasks.title: Shell command allowlist and denylist implement
acceptance_criteria: Policy classifies commands into safe, approval_required, denied and unknown.
Dangerous patterns such as destructive filesystem operations are denied or approval-required by default.
Command policy cannot loosen Hub policy decisions.
Tests cover allowlist, denylist, unknown command and path escape cases.

tasks.title: Approval-gated command execution implement
acceptance_criteria: Command execution requires policy allow or matching approval for approval-required commands.
Executor captures stdout, stderr, exit code, duration and environment summary.
Executor runs with constrained cwd and environment.
Tests verify denied command does not execute.

tasks.title: Shell explain and repair hints add
acceptance_criteria: Worker can explain a command and likely effects without executing it.
Worker can summarize failure output and suggest next safe commands as plans.
Repair hints remain plan artifacts unless approved for execution.
Tests cover failed test command and missing dependency command.

tasks.title: Worker model provider interface add
acceptance_criteria: Interface supports OpenAI-compatible providers and local providers through configuration.
Interface records model id, provider, base_url label, timeout and prompt template version in result metadata.
Interface supports deterministic mock provider for tests.
Missing provider produces degraded no-LLM mode instead of crash where operation supports fallback.

tasks.title: External coding tool adapter interface define
acceptance_criteria: Interface defines capabilities, plan, propose_patch, run_tests and optional apply_patch methods.
Adapters return Ananta artifacts and cannot directly commit/apply changes without approval path.
Interface declares whether adapter is native, optional, experimental or unavailable.
Document explains external tool outputs are untrusted.

tasks.title: Aider adapter prototype add
acceptance_criteria: Adapter can be disabled when aider is not installed.
Adapter exposes plan/propose_patch capabilities without becoming required dependency.
Adapter output is captured and converted to PatchArtifact or degraded result.
Tests use mocked subprocess calls.

tasks.title: ShellGPT-style adapter prototype add
acceptance_criteria: Adapter is optional and disabled when shellgpt-compatible tool is unavailable.
Adapter is used only for command explanation/planning by default.
Adapter cannot execute commands without going through Ananta command executor.
Tests use mocked subprocess calls.

tasks.title: Copilot CLI and OpenCode experimental adapters add
acceptance_criteria: Adapters are marked experimental and disabled unless explicitly configured.
Copilot CLI adapter requires bring-your-own local auth and is never an OSS default dependency.
OpenCode adapter handles unavailable or archived/upstream-changing tool state gracefully.
Adapters cannot bypass patch artifact, approval or audit path.

tasks.title: Worker verification service implement
acceptance_criteria: Verification service can evaluate patch/test command results and produce VerificationArtifact.
Verification distinguishes passed, failed, skipped, degraded and inconclusive.
Verification records commands, outputs, exit codes and artifact refs.
Tests cover passing test, failing test and skipped verification.

tasks.title: Worker audit and trace metadata implement
acceptance_criteria: Worker results include trace_id, task_id, capability_id, context_hash, policy_decision_ref and approval_ref where applicable.
Patch and command artifacts include stable hashes.
Trace metadata supports todo.security.json TraceBundle expectations.
Tests verify missing trace metadata fails for execution-capable paths.

tasks.title: Worker redaction and secret hygiene add
acceptance_criteria: Worker redacts likely secrets from logs, prompts, command output summaries and visual/e2e reports where practical.
Worker avoids injecting API keys or tokens into external tool subprocess environments unless explicitly required and allowed.
Tests include token-like, path-like and false-positive cases.
Redaction failures can block release evidence publication.

tasks.title: Worker degradation states implement
acceptance_criteria: Worker reports unavailable model, unavailable external tool, missing git repo, denied policy and missing approval as distinct states.
Degraded states are user-readable and machine-readable.
Degraded states never masquerade as success.
Tests cover each major degraded state.

tasks.title: Native worker unit test suite add
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover workspace, patch planner, diff builder, command planner, command executor, verification and trace metadata.
Tests use deterministic fixtures and mock model providers.
Tests do not require external Aider/OpenCode/Copilot/ShellGPT tools.
Tests are included in release gate when worker runtime is claimed.

tasks.title: Tiny repo patch E2E add
acceptance_criteria: E2E runs on a tiny fixture repo and produces plan, patch, test result, verification and trace artifacts.
E2E uses mocked model output by default.
E2E verifies original working tree is not modified before approval.
E2E stores evidence compatible with todo.e2e-dogfood.json.

tasks.title: Failing test repair E2E add
acceptance_criteria: E2E simulates failing test output and generates repair plan or patch artifact.
Repair path goes through same policy/approval/trace expectations as normal patch flow.
E2E verifies failure output is summarized and not hidden.
E2E stores repair artifact and verification result.

tasks.title: Denied command E2E add
acceptance_criteria: E2E submits a dangerous command request and verifies it is denied before execution.
E2E proves no command side effects occurred.
Policy denied artifact and trace metadata are produced.
E2E includes CLI/TUI snapshot hooks where available.

tasks.title: Worker release gate integration add
acceptance_criteria: Release gate runs worker unit tests and core E2E flows when native worker runtime is claimed.
Gate fails on policy bypass, direct main-tree mutation, missing trace metadata or denied command execution.
Gate treats experimental external adapters as optional unless explicitly enabled.
Gate prints worker evidence/report paths.

tasks.title: Worker documentation and comparison write
acceptance_criteria: Docs explain native worker concepts, modes, safety boundaries and golden path usage.
Comparison explains what Ananta borrows conceptually from Aider/OpenCode/ShellGPT/Copilot CLI and what it deliberately does differently.
Adapter docs explain optional installation/configuration and why adapters are not default dependencies.
Docs include honest limitations and unsupported flows.

tasks.title: Controlled autonomous worker loop implement
acceptance_criteria: Loop implements observe -> plan -> patch -> test -> inspect -> repair -> retest -> summarize.
Loop consumes Task + ContextEnvelope + policy + approval.
Loop produces intermediate artifacts per iteration.
Loop never bypasses policy or approval.
Loop state is externally observable.

tasks.title: Worker loop budgets and stop conditions add
acceptance_criteria: Supports max_iterations, max_patch_attempts, max_runtime_seconds.
Stops on policy_denied, approval_required, repeated_failure.
Stops on no_progress_detected.
Stop reason is recorded in trace.

tasks.title: Worker progress events and intermediate artifacts add
acceptance_criteria: Each iteration emits progress events.
Events include phase, iteration, artifacts.
Intermediate results visible.
No silent internal loop state.

tasks.title: OpenCode-like autonomous coding E2E add
acceptance_criteria: Worker fixes a failing test in iterative loop.
Loop stops correctly on failure/approval.
No uncontrolled execution.
Artifacts + trace prove full loop.

execution_order_summary: Stage 1: Define worker architecture, request/result schemas and capabilities.
Stage 2: Build isolated workspace/worktree and patch artifact flow.
Stage 3: Add RAG-aware file selection, prompt assembly and native patch proposal.
Stage 4: Add ShellGPT-style command planning and approval-gated command execution.
Stage 5: Add model provider interface and optional external tool adapters.
Stage 6: Add verification, trace, redaction and degradation states.
Stage 7: Add unit tests, E2E dogfood evidence, release-gate integration and docs.

AW-M4.title: Shell command planning and execution controls

AW-M5.title: Model provider and external tool adapters

notes: AW-M3, AW-M4 and AW-M5 (AW-T10..AW-T22) were implemented and marked done.
Rollups were synchronized to reflect 22 done tasks and 5 done milestones.
Stage counters keep remaining bundles (AW-M6..AW-M8) explicitly visible.

summary_notes: This track should come after todo.tests.json, todo.security.json and enough of todo.more-generic.json to provide stable ContextEnvelope, Policy, Approval, Artifact and Trace primitives.
The worker should be Ananta-native first. External tools are adapters, not architecture owners.
Aider is the strongest inspiration for patch/git workflow; ShellGPT is useful for shell command UX; Copilot CLI is optional/experimental because it depends on a commercial service and local auth.
The first real deliverable should be a tiny repo patch E2E that proves plan -> patch artifact -> test -> verification -> approval-gated apply.
Track metadata now includes per-stage counters aligned with milestone scope.

end_summaries.title: Analyseergebnis
items: Die Aufgabenliste umfasst 36 Tasks (AW-T01..AW-T36) und 8 Milestones (AW-M1..AW-M8).
Die vorhandenen Rollups waren veraltet (32/7 statt 36/8).

end_summaries.title: Korrekturen
items: AW-T10..AW-T22 wurden auf done gesetzt; AW-M3, AW-M4 und AW-M5 auf done synchronisiert.
tasks_status_summary, critical_path und milestone-Counter wurden auf den neuen Zwischenstand aktualisiert.

end_summaries.title: Verbesserungen/Erweiterungen
items: analysis_checks ergänzt, um Korrekturgrund und Datenstand transparent zu dokumentieren.

end_summaries.title: Status am Ende
items: Aktueller Umsetzungsstand: 14 todo, 0 in_progress, 0 blocked, 22 done (61.11%).
